name: Texas Muslim Womens Foundation Inc
mission: Founded in 2005, Texas Muslim Women's Foundation (TMWF) aims to empower, promote and support all women and their families, with a special focus on North Texas. We are an equal opportunity provider. We engage all members of the community – women, men, seniors, families and youth through our various programs. We provide social and family violence services that foster peace and well-being in the home through our Peace in the Home Services and Peaceful Oasis Shelter. In addition, TMWF also arranges leadership skills development for Muslim youth to inspire the next generation to build a promising future for our society and educational programs for Muslim and non-Muslim community members to learn about topics essential to individual and community welfare.
theoryOfChange: The Texas Muslim Women's Foundation (TMWF) has a published report titled "From the Roots of Trauma to the Flowering of Trauma-Informed Care." This document details TMWF's four-year project (starting in 2014) to design and implement culturally-sensitive trauma-informed programming for domestic violence survivors and their children. The report charts their process of becoming a trauma-informed agency, which involved learning about types of trauma and trauma-informed care, assessing and enhancing existing culturally-sensitive practices, training staff, and collaborating with researchers to document and build evidence-based practice. TMWF articulates a model that integrates culturally-sensitive services and trauma-informed advocacy, which they invite others to learn from and replicate.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcome data in Form 990 filings and a documented Theory of Change, though cost-per-beneficiary metrics are not fully public.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: TMWF utilizes a direct service model to move survivors from crisis to stability through emergency shelter, followed by transitional housing and social support.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Verified housing numbers ensure that donor funds are successfully transitioning families out of dangerous environments.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 17.3% growth rate and expanding housing programs, TMWF has significant capacity to absorb new funding for shelter expansion.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="3">98.98.66666666666667/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, including a <cite id="10">perfect 100 for leadership and culture</cite>.
TMWF operates with a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.04 per $1 raised</cite>, far outperforming industry standards for domestic nonprofits.
In 2024, the foundation provided critical safety for <cite id="11">137 emergency shelter residents</cite> and <cite id="12">216 non-residential domestic violence clients</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: TMWF's strength lies in its direct service delivery model, which is backed by a <cite id="13">strong and documented Theory of Change</cite>. Their financial health is resilient, maintaining <cite id="14">3.7 months of working capital</cite> to ensure continuity of shelter services. The organization's growth is steady, with revenue increasing from <cite id="15">$3.39 million in 2021 to over $4.66 million in 2023</cite>, allowing for the expansion of programs like 'Food 4 Good' and senior supportive housing.
rich_narrative.summary: The Texas Muslim Women's Foundation (TMWF) is a specialized social service agency that has been <cite id="1">operating since 2005</cite> to provide culturally sensitive support for women and families. Based in North Texas, the organization addresses critical needs through a comprehensive model that includes <cite id="2">emergency shelters, transitional housing, and social services</cite>. TMWF is a significant institution within the American Muslim philanthropic landscape, maintaining a <cite id="3">98.66666666666667/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>. In the 2023 fiscal year, the foundation managed <cite id="4">$4,665,216 in total revenue</cite>, demonstrating a robust <cite id="5">3-year revenue CAGR of 17.3%</cite>. Their operations are highly efficient, characterized by a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.04 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions reach those in crisis. The organization's impact is quantifiable; in 2023 alone, they <cite id="7">housed 186 survivors in shelters</cite> and supported <cite id="8">204 individuals through transitional or rapid re-housing programs</cite>. Beyond housing, TMWF provides a safety net for <cite id="9">553 non-residential clients</cite>, offering a holistic approach to domestic violence and poverty alleviation that respects the cultural and religious nuances of the community it serves.
